title: achnatherum calamagrostis subsp. mesatlasicum
intro: Achnatherum calamagrostis subsp. mesatlasicum, also known as Sand Grama or Sand Bunchgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to the southwestern United States. It is a type of perennial grass that grows in sandy or well-drained areas, with narrow leaves and a distinctive bunching growth habit. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Achnatherum calamagrostis subsp. mesatlasicum may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues.
